So, three pillars. Fitness restriction accountability. Fitness-wise they're going to work out three times a week with a trainer. That's going to be a big benefit. That's going to cater to their fitness levels so you can explain to them whether they are advanced or they are a beginner. We have two levels of programming and you can go from level one to level two and then ideally you want to get as many of your exercises at level two. So, it's going to be the fitness component to fitness restriction accountability for their transformation. We also include at-home workouts so that if they are on vacation, etc., they are away or they can't make it into the gym. We can still close the sale because we are like, listen even if you are away for a week you can do the at-home workouts. No problem. You have to make sure that this works for you in the long run. If you take a vacation it means it's not like you stop being in shape. You stop being into fitness. So, they also have at-home workouts which helps you in the sale but also really does help them. That also is a big component of one of the sales innovations that we have recently done that has massively exploded the bottom lines for the gyms that we work with. And they will have a coach who is checking in with them there as well. So, they will have their own personal accountability coach which I should add to this but I am telling you. Personal accountability coach is checking in with them about their weight and then making adjustments to their meal plan as needed to make sure that they are continuing to lose weight or continue to move towards their goal. And then lastly, there is an online accountability group which has two benefits. One is you have the benefit of the alumni, people who have been through the program and can give the conviction to the new people saying like, oh, I know you are on week one. Like, it sucks, don't worry, make it through it. And then you also have the people who are going through it together side by side saying, wow, yesterday's workout sucked. That was my second workout. That was my second workout too. So, you have both levels of community. One is kind of more aspirational. Like, these people have been, you know, they are further along in the journey and the people who are walking alongside you doing it too. So, it is kind of both levels which a lot of people really, really appreciate and need.
